name: onedrive-blade-mcp
title: Microsoft OneDrive
description: OneDrive and SharePoint file operations via Microsoft Graph API — browse, read, search, upload, share, delta sync
tagline: Browse, search, and sync files across OneDrive and SharePoint
author: groupthink-dev

env.name: ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET
env.description: Azure AD app client secret (optional — for client_credentials flow)

env.name: ONEDRIVE_WRITE_ENABLED
env.description: Enable write operations (upload, move, delete, share)

setup.help_text: 1. Register an app at Azure Portal → App registrations. 2. Set platform to 'Mobile and desktop applications'. 3. Add Files.ReadWrite.All and Sites.ReadWrite.All permissions. 4. Copy Tenant ID and Client ID. 5. On first run, complete the device code flow.
setup.blurb: Browse, read, search, and manage files across OneDrive and SharePoint with delta sync for incremental change tracking.

fields.label: Auth Mode
fields.help: Use 'device_code' for interactive setup, 'client_credentials' for headless/CI

prerequisites: Azure AD app registration with Microsoft Graph API permissions

highlights.text: Browse — navigate folders and files across OneDrive and SharePoint
Search — full-text search across all drives and sites
Upload — create and update files with write gate
Share — generate sharing links with scoped permissions
Delta sync — incremental change tracking for efficient polling
Dual auth — device code for interactive, client credentials for headless

readme:
### What it does

OneDrive Blade MCP gives your AI agent access to files stored in OneDrive and SharePoint through the Microsoft Graph API. Your agent can browse folder hierarchies, read file contents, search across drives, upload documents, create sharing links, and track incremental changes with delta sync.

One MCP server covers personal OneDrive, OneDrive for Business, and SharePoint document libraries.

### Delta sync

The delta sync capability tracks changes since the last sync token, returning only new, modified, or deleted items. This is far more efficient than listing entire directories and is ideal for agents that need to monitor a folder for new documents or detect file changes between sessions.

### Token efficiency

Graph API file metadata includes dozens of fields (creation info, hashes, parentReference chains, SharePoint-specific facets). Each tool's concise formatter extracts name, path, size, modified date, and download URL — dropping internal IDs and nested metadata. Directory listings stay compact even for large folders.

### Safety model

File operations follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — browse, read, search, list, delta
- **Writes** require `ONEDRIVE_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — upload, move, delete, share
- **Destructive operations** are double-gated — delete files, revoke shares

scenarios.label: Find and retrieve a document
scenarios.body: Search across OneDrive and SharePoint for a document by name or content, browse the containing folder for context, and read the file contents for analysis.

scenarios.label: Monitor a folder for new files
scenarios.body: Use delta sync to check a shared folder for files added since the last check, list the new items with metadata, and read any documents that match specific criteria.

scenarios.label: Upload and share a report
scenarios.body: Upload a generated report to a specific OneDrive folder, create a sharing link with view-only permissions, and verify the file appears in the target directory.
